title: alopecurus antarcticus f. bracteatus
intro: Alopecurus antarcticus f. bracteatus, also known as the Antarctic foxtail, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Alopecurus antarcticus f. bracteatus include relieving digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, it is prepared as a tea or tincture, and may also be used in combination with other herbs to create a medicinal decoction.
